title: Route differentiation controller phase
summary: Lane 1 should protect the next tactile route wave by extracting one small living-world route-differentiation seam away from game.ts and syncing review-drop packaging truth.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the route-differentiation controller split handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The next route-feel win should not become another game.ts pileup.

execution_order.goal: Implement one route-differentiation helper seam and the review-drop packaging note.
execution_order.why_now: The current gameplay scaffolding is strong enough to protect with a small extraction before another tactile wave lands.
The pass only matters if it lowers coordination risk without changing live behavior.

guardrails: Do not add a new support slot, planner shell, or route HUD.
Prefer a pure or mostly-pure helper seam over another broad state object.
Keep the review-drop note short and practical.

main_280_focus.preferred_seam: one living-world route-differentiation helper that sits between the current support-specific targeting logic and the route-facing live-variant logic
recommended_hotspots: game.ts hand-lens notebook-fit checks in inspectEntity and getNearestInspectable
main_280_focus.docs_note: Add one short reviewer-facing note to omit node_modules from external review or playable archive drops.
